Report_0657.pdf: Application Fraud Case - KARTHIK - App ID: 9859651257 (Signature
Fraud Type: Signature Forgery. Current Status: Pending.

Internal watchlist and previous applications screened - no further matches.
Mail sent to CIU recommending decline of application and account monitoring flag enabled.
Review covered application documents, KYC records, and transaction monitoring alerts related to the case.

Captioned case logged under CARDS_AFTER_MURABAHA-PDC scheme.
Applicant (KARTHIK) contacted. Irregularities linked to Signature Forgery identified.

Applicant (KARTHIK) contacted. Irregularities linked to Signature Forgery identified.
Signature mismatch and KYC deviation observed.

Mail sent to CIU recommending decline of application and account monitoring flag enabled.
Final status: Pending. Control actions applied as per policy.
Based on the investigation conducted, the case against applicant KARTHIK (App ID: 9859651257) has been conclusively
assessed as an instance of Signature Forgery. The findings indicate clear documentary discrepancies, signature
mismatches, and KYC deviations that substantiate the fraud classification.
The Fraud Risk Management (FRM) team, led by Investigator Anita Sharma and reviewed by Anita Sharma, undertook a
thorough examination of all application documents, KYC records, and internal watchlist databases. No additional exposure or
linked accounts were identified during the screening process.
Appropriate control actions have been applied in line with the Bank's fraud management policy. A recommendation for
decline of the application has been communicated to the Credit Investigation Unit (CIU), and an account monitoring flag has
been enabled to prevent any future recurrence.
The case is currently recorded as Pending. All relevant stakeholders have been notified, and the matter has been escalated
in accordance with regulatory and internal reporting obligations. A Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) reference
STR-AUTO-200320191HD has been filed as required.
The investigation team recommends continued vigilance and periodic review of verification controls to mitigate similar fraud
risks in future application processing cycles.
Strengthen verification procedures and monitoring alerts.

complaint_3pages.pdf: To: The Branch Manager / Head of Compliance & Internal Audit
Subject: Unexplained Deduction of AED 45,000 - Request for Immediate Investigation
I, Mohammed Al Rashidi, hold a current account (No. ****-****- 7842) with your esteemed institution. I have been
a loyal customer for over seven years and have maintained this account in good standing. It is with great
concern and distress that I bring to your notice an incident that has caused me significant financial harm and has
shaken my trust in the bank.
On 15 January 2025, while reviewing my monthly statement via the bank's mobile application, I noticed an
outward transfer of AED 45,000 (Forty-Five Thousand UAE Dirhams) recorded on 14 January 2025 under
transaction reference TRF00821. I have absolutely no recollection of initiating or authorising this transaction. I
did not visit the branch on that date, nor did I use any online or telephone banking facility to request such a
I immediately contacted the bank's customer service helpline. After placing me on hold for an extended period,
the agent confirmed that the transaction was processed as an in-branch instruction and was handled by Mr.
Kharim (Staff ID: 09800). I was then advised to visit the branch with my identification documents to file a formal
Fig 1 - Scanned transaction voucher retrieved from branch records. Customer disputes the instruction reflected in this document.
Note: 'Authorised By' field lists Mr. Kharim (Staff ID 09800).
This document constitutes a formal complaint. Retain for legal reference.
On 16 January 2025, I visited the branch and met with the Customer Service Supervisor. I was shown a
physical payment instruction document that was purportedly used as the basis for processing the transaction.
Upon examination, I was alarmed to find that while the document bore what appeared to be a version of my
name, the handwritten elements did not correspond to anything I have ever written or submitted to the bank.
The supervisor initially seemed reluctant to acknowledge the discrepancy. However, when I pressed further and
presented my own signature specimens available on my existing bank mandate, a visible difference was
apparent. I am formally requesting that the original document be submitted to a qualified handwriting expert for
I have serious concerns about the conduct of Mr. Kharim (Staff ID: 09800) in this matter. The transaction was
processed without any telephonic verification, OTP confirmation, or call-back to my registered mobile number -
all of which are standard security procedures mandated for high-value outward transfers. The bypassing of
these controls raises significant questions about how this transaction was approved at the teller/operations level.
I am not making any accusation lightly. However, I urge the compliance and internal audit team to review all
transactions processed by Staff ID 09800 during the period 1 January 2025 to 15 January 2025 to determine
* This statement is for reference only. Dispute this entry at your nearest branch.
Fig 2 - Official account statement excerpt showing the disputed AED 45,000 debit on 14-Jan-2025. Prior balance was AED 60,000;
Exhibit C - System-Generated Debit Advice (Customer Copy)
Fig 3 - System-generated debit advice issued to customer. Note 'DISPUTED' stamp applied by branch upon customer's challenge.
This document was provided to the complainant during the 16 January 2025 branch visit.
This document constitutes a formal complaint. Retain for legal reference.
· I did not visit the branch on 14 January 2025 and made no transaction requests on that date.
· An amount of AED 45,000 was debited from my account via reference TRF00821 without my instruction.
· The transaction was processed by Mr. Kharim (Staff ID: 09800) at the Dubai - Business Bay Branch.
· The authorisation document shown to me does not reflect my genuine handwriting or consent.
· No verification call, OTP, or confirmation was sent to my registered mobile number (+971 50 XXX XXXX).
· This transaction has caused a direct financial loss of AED 45,000 to my household.
1. Immediate written acknowledgement of this complaint and assignment of a case officer.
2. Preservation of all branch CCTV footage, transaction logs, and system access records for 14 January 2025.
3. Independent handwriting/forensic review of the instruction document used to process TRF00821.
4. Suspension of Mr. Kharim (Staff ID: 09800) from transaction processing duties pending investigation.
5. Full audit of all transactions handled by Staff ID 09800 for the month of January 2025.
6. Reversal of the AED 45,000 debit and reinstatement of the full account balance, or equivalent compensation.
7. Written response with investigation findings within 10 working days of receipt of this complaint.
Should this matter not be resolved satisfactorily within the stipulated timeframe, I hereby reserve the right to
escalate my complaint to the UAE Central Bank Consumer Protection Department and to seek legal counsel
for the recovery of my funds and any consequential damages arising from this incident. I may also file a report
with the relevant law enforcement authorities if the investigation indicates deliberate misconduct.
I declare that all information provided in this complaint is true and accurate to the best of my knowledge. I am
prepared to cooperate fully with any investigation and to provide any additional documentation required.
Enclosures: Copy of Emirates ID | Account Statement (Jan 2025) | Transaction Voucher (Exhibit A) | Debit
Yours sincerely, Mohammed Al Rashidi | Dated: 15 January 2025
This document constitutes a formal complaint. Retain for legal reference.

(anonymous)
FORMAL COMPLAINT – FINANCIAL LOSS CLAIM
Ref: CMP-2025-0114-A | Confidential
FORMAL COMPLAINT – FINANCIAL LOSS CLAIM
FORMAL COMPLAINT – FINANCIAL LOSS CLAIM
Ref: CMP-2025-0114-A | Confidential
Ref: CMP-2025-0114-A | Confidential
FORMAL COMPLAINT REGARDING UNAUTHORISED ACCOUNT ACTIVITY
Submitted to: Branch Manager / Compliance Department
Complainant Name:
Mohammed Al Rashidi
Date of Complaint:
15 January 2025
Account No:
****-****-7842
Complaint Ref:
CMP-2025-0114-A
Contact:
+971 50 XXX XXXX
Staff Concerned:
Mr. Kharim | Staff ID: 09800
Subject: Formal Complaint – Unexplained Debit of AED 45,000 from My Account
Dear Sir / Madam,
I am writing to formally bring to your attention a deeply concerning matter involving my bank account and a transaction I did not authorise. On 14 January 2025, an amount of AED 45,000 (Forty-Five Thousand UAE Dirhams) was debited from my account without my knowledge, consent, or any verbal or written instruction from my side.
Upon reviewing my account statement, I discovered this transaction processed under reference TRF00821. When I visited the branch to investigate, I was informed by another staff member that the transaction was processed by Mr. Kharim (Staff ID: 09800), who is currently assigned to the accounts and transactions desk. I was shown a payment instruction document purportedly bearing my authorisation — however, I categorically deny providing any such instruction. The document presented to me does not reflect my handwriting or consent in any recognisable manner.
I have retained copies of my original identification documents and account records. I am requesting an immediate internal investigation into this matter and urge the Bank to preserve all records, CCTV footage, and access logs related to this transaction.
Evidence – Exhibit A: Transaction Voucher (Branch Copy – Disputed)
Fig 1 – Scanned copy of transaction voucher (OCR verified). Customer denies authorisation.
Immediate Actions Requested:
Freeze or investigate the beneficiary account that received the AED 45,000 transfer.
Conduct a full audit of all transactions processed by Staff ID 09800 (Mr. Kharim) during January 2025.
Preserve all branch CCTV footage and system access logs for 14 January 2025.
Initiate a reversal or recovery of the disputed amount pending investigation outcome.
Provide a written acknowledgement of this complaint within 3 working days.
Evidence – Exhibit B: Account Statement Excerpt (January 2025)
Fig 2 – Bank account statement showing unauthorised AED 45,000 debit on 14-Jan-2025.
Additional Details & Declaration:
I wish to state on record that I have never delegated authority to any bank employee — including Mr. Kharim (Staff ID: 09800) — to process outward transfers on my behalf. I was not present at the branch on 14 January 2025, and I have proof of my whereabouts for that date should it be required by the investigating authority.
I am deeply distressed by this incident and hold the bank accountable for the safeguarding of my funds. If the matter is not resolved promptly, I reserve the right to escalate this complaint to the UAE Central Bank Consumer Protection Department and seek legal redress.
Complainant Signature:
Date:
Witness (if present):
15 / 01 / 2025
Mohammed Al Rashidi
Name & Designation
This complaint has been prepared in good faith and all supporting documents are available upon request. Please acknowledge receipt and advise on next steps.
Yours sincerely, Mohammed Al Rashidi | Account No: ****-****-7842
This document constitutes a formal complaint. Retain for legal reference.
